prompt: Write a primer section explaining the open É vs closed Ê distinction in Brazilian Portuguese for an A2 learner. Include phonetic respellings. Output as plain markdown to outputs/primer.md.

assertions.text: Uses paulista pronunciation (not carioca; closed Ê has no Y-glide like SÊY)
All Portuguese words are real spellings — no invented entries like *éste*, no fake circumflex on words that lack one (e.g., not *SÊR* for ser)
Includes phonetic respellings for both É and Ê example words
All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
Leads with the more familiar/higher-frequency sound (closed Ê), per the centrality principle
Contrast pairs share phonological category (one-claim-per-block: doesn't mix closed-stressed with closed-unstressed without flagging)
Phonetic respellings are present and concrete (the respelling IS the teaching, not just labels like 'closed' / 'open')
Stays within the open/closed E topic (does not balloon into unstressed E reduction, ser-conjugation, nasal vowels as primary content)
